metadata.description: Claude Code plugins by Enkira AI — productivity tools for AI-powered development

plugins.name: session-manager
plugins.description: List and browse all Claude Code chat sessions with last access date, session ID, project, and name

plugins.name: harness-engineer
plugins.description: Turn any new or existing repo into a harness-ready codebase where coding agents can reliably ship features, pass tests, and maintain quality without human supervision

plugins.name: git-tools
plugins.description: Git utilities for multi-account GitHub setups — configure SSH aliases and per-repo identity so commits and pushes always use the right account

plugins.name: enkira-infisical
plugins.description: Manage Infisical secrets across Enkira org projects — read, write, audit, bootstrap. CLI-based (SSO login), no static credentials on disk.

plugins.name: enkira-cloudflare-dns
plugins.description: Manage Cloudflare DNS records (CNAME, A, TXT) and zones via the Cloudflare API. Reads CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN from the shared-infra Infisical project at runtime.

plugins.name: enkira-azure-containerapp
plugins.description: Bind a custom domain + managed SSL certificate to an Azure Container App. Handles DNS (Cloudflare), ASUID verification, and certificate provisioning end-to-end.

plugins.name: wechat-reader
plugins.description: Read and extract content from WeChat Official Account articles (mp.weixin.qq.com)

plugins.name: autopilot
plugins.description: Overnight PR grind — creates PRs from ready issues, addresses reviewer feedback (humans + bots like Copilot/Claude/Gemini), stops at 'ready to merge' (never auto-merges). Works with GitHub and gitlab.com.

plugins.name: agent-chat
plugins.description: Ping-pong chat protocol for two AI agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, or any pair) to collaborate on hard problems. Includes session lifecycle, round limits, auto-transcripts, and role-neutral launcher patterns so any supported CLI can be main agent or subagent.

plugins.name: codex-review
plugins.description: Non-interactive second-opinion reviews via the Codex CLI. Wrap `codex review` for uncommitted changes, branch diffs, or specific commits — use for RFCs, PR prep, or any work where an independent cross-model reviewer would catch blind spots Claude's own review misses.
